  "findings": [
    "New CMU veneer exhibits light white powdery haze (efflorescence) after winter weather.",
    "Mortar joints are properly tooled and intact.",
    "Weep holes are open and unobstructed.",
    "Interior humidity levels are normal, indicating no active internal water intrusion."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Monitor the wall to see if the efflorescence naturally dissipates with dry weather.",
    "If cosmetic removal is desired, dry brush the surface or wash with clean water and a stiff bristle brush."
  ],
  "rationale": "The observed condition is normal, temporary 'new building' efflorescence caused by the drying out of residual construction moisture. Because the joints are intact, weeps are clear, and interior humidity is normal, there is no systemic water intrusion or structural defect. No corrective masonry rework is warranted.",
  "references": [
    "BIA Technical Notes on Brick Construction 23 - Efflorescence Causes and Prevention",
    "BIA Technical Notes on Brick Construction 23A - Efflorescence Testing and Correction"
  ]
}
